text: on the task, her movements slow and deliberate, but she looked up at him the moment he stepped into the room. She smiled, wrinkles bunching at the corners of her eyes . Her hair was gray, now; she’d panicked when she found her first gray hair, locking herself in the bathroom for more than an hour before he finally forced the door open to ensure she was okay. She’d taken it as a sign of her mortality, convinced he’d leave her at any moment . But she was as beautiful to him now as when he first saw her. Even more so, after the life they’d shared . “Tabby found some flowers for you,” he said, holding up the bundle . “She could’ve brought them herself,” she said. Ronin didn’t miss the trembling in her hand as she set the knife down. “She only lives three houses away, and I would have loved to see her .” “She and Dan are helping with repairs on the wall, which means she was already running late. Tomorrow is her free day. She said she’ll come by .” Lara stared down at the jellied bread, smiling. “I’m so proud of her. Of all of them .” “They had the best mother this side of the Dust,” Ronin replied, smiling as well. Though Tabitha had been Lara’s only natural-born child — complications during the birth had made it too risky to try again — they’d adopted and raised three other children, all loved just as much as Tabby. Samuel, Melissa, and Amanda . Lara chuckled and lifted her arms toward him. “Are you just going to stand there, or are you going to come say hello ?” Ronin crossed the room and embraced her, gently but warmly, running a hand along her silver braid . “Hello,” he said, and dipped his head to kiss her . “Hello,” she returned. “Would you put them in water ?” “Of course.” He placed the flowers on the counter, picked up her bread, and took her arm. She leaned on him as she walked to the table, where he pulled a chair out with his boot and helped her ease into it . “You’re always so good to me .” “It’s no less than you deserve, Lara Brooks .” “Still calling me Brooks after all this time.” She shook her head, but there was mirth in her eyes as she picked up the bread and took a bite . Ronin found a vase for the flowers and filled it with water from the sink. “I’ve told you already, just because I married you without having a surname of my own doesn’t mean you get to drop yours.” Returning to the table, he placed the vase at its center . She’d only taken two bites when she set the bread down and raised a hand to her forehead. “It’s too early to be so tired .” “Sunset is in less than an hour,” Ronin said. “And when have you ever passed up an opportunity for me to take you to bed, anyway ?” “We can go upstairs, as long as you promise not to rip my shirt this time,” she replied, but her smile was strained, her face too pale . “ Come , then .” He pulled out her chair and lifted her into his arms. Lara’s weight had always been slight to him, but she was thinner now than ever before. She slid her arms around his neck and rested her head on his shoulder as he carried her up to the bedroom . Ronin set her on her feet beside the bed and she took his face between her hands, kissing him. He returned the kiss and undressed her. His hands brushed lightly over her skin — so changed, and yet so familiar — lingering on the stretch marks, the scars. It was evidence of a hard-fought life, made all the more precious by its struggles . “You remain the most beautiful thing I have ever seen,” he said . “You’ve always made me feel that way .” He helped her into her nightgown and pulled back the covers. She slipped into bed slowly. Ronin kicked off his boots and changed out of his dirty clothes, putting on a plain shirt and a pair of shorts. He climbed onto the bed and drew her into his arms. Her body still fit against his perfectly . Lara’s light breath tickled the sensors on his skin. He took her braid in one hand, absently running his fingertips over its length . “I never thought I’d have this life,” she said softly . “Neither did I. It is more than I could have ever hoped .” She reached up and brushed her fingers over his jaw. “Even after all these years, I haven’t had enough time to love you .” Her words pierced him; they were too final, too true. He settled a hand on her cheek and ran his thumbs over the fine wrinkles near her eye. Ten thousand years wouldn’t be enough, but this…it was still too soon . “We’ll have years more, still,” he said . “Bots always say what they mean,” Lara said, “but that doesn’t make it the truth .” “ Lara , I — ” “Shh.” She placed a finger over his lips. Her smile was warm, though her eyes were tired. “Will you stay with me while I sleep ?” “You know I always do,” he replied . “And you know I always ask .” She relaxed, her weight settling against him, and soon her breathing evened out. He measured time by the slow, faint beat of her heart, pulling up memories from their lives together . Her sunburn after her first day working in the fields, despite covering herself. The joyous smile she’d worn the first time she held Tabitha and the tears that followed. Her patience with the townsfolk when they came to her — the mother of the revolution
